feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP S/4 Hana Cloud: Testing Form Development
entry.content:
Team,

I am trying to download the .XML file that includes the sample data from Purchase Order using syntax -'#&&DOWNLOAD_FORM_DATA_XML&&#'. but getting below error and upon continuing this error and bypassing using continue nothing happenned.

 

Followed the below thread - 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-s-4-hana-cloud-testing-form-developments-in-adobe-livecycle-designer/ba-p/13416953

entry.title: Is PO Item Text output within form can be controlled at Purchase organization level ?
entry.content: In S4 HANA Public cloud , Is there is a functionality where we can restrict printing a Purchase order item text in the form layout with respect to Purchase Organization ?

entry.title: Get Delivery Header Text in S4HANA Cloud custom CDS view
entry.content:
Team,

How do i get the Long Text stored within Delivery's Header textid within Custom CDS View?

entry.title: Screen personas S4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Team,

I would like to ask if i want to restrict posting a movement type from an app called 'Warehouse Monitor' based on some specific users.

Is it possible to cater this via Screen persona?

entry.title: SAP SCREEN PERSONAS - SAP EWM WAREHOUSE MONITOR
entry.content: Wanted to disable button within app - Warehouse Monitor based on movement type that too applicable for some users. Is it possible in S/4 HANA Public Cloud?

entry.title: In this custom logic " YY1_LGC_PO_AGENT_DET " It says "BPidentificationNumber" is an unknown column
entry.content:
In this custom logic " YY1_LGC_PO_AGENT_DET " CDS Entity I_BUSINESSUSER is deprecated and we replaced with CDS Entity I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC . But  in this custom logic we applied "WHERE BPIdentificationNumber" operation and it says "BPIdentificationNumber is an unknown column"


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: DEBUG Custom logic S4HANA CLOUD
entry.content: I just wanted to understand in S4HANA Cloud public edition , if I need to debug the logic written inside BADI within CUSTOM LOGIC app. How could I perform ? Also i don't have ADT luxury.

entry.title: Embracing SAP Clean Core: A Strategic Imperative for Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving digital landscape, enterprises are constantly seeking ways to streamline operations, enhance agility, and drive innovation. SAP Clean Core emerges as a pivotal strategy in this quest, offering a path to a simplified, efficient, and future-ready enterprise core. This blog post explores the significance of SAP Clean Core, its alignment with SAP Activate methodology, the synergies with RISE with SAP, the transformative capabilities of S/4HANA, and the role of KTern.AI in facilitating this journey.

SAP Activate Methodology: The Blueprint for Success

The SAP Activate methodology is a flexible, agile framework designed to guide businesses through the implementation and deployment of SAP solutions. It encompasses best practices, guided configurations, and a clear methodology to ensure project success. By leveraging SAP Activate, organizations can accelerate their SAP S/4HANA implementations, ensuring a clean core that is devoid of unnecessary customizations and complexity.

RISE with SAP: Elevating Business Transformation

RISE with SAP is a comprehensive offering that simplifies the migration of ERP systems to the cloud. It encapsulates cloud solutions, infrastructure, and services, providing a seamless transition path. With RISE with SAP, businesses can benefit from embedded intelligence, real-time insights, and advanced technologies, all of which contribute to maintaining a clean core and fostering business resilience.

SAP S/4HANA: The Heart of Digital Transformation

At the core of the digital transformation journey lies SAP S/4HANA, SAP’s most advanced ERP suite. Designed to run on the in-memory SAP HANA database, SAP S/4HANA integrates functions from lines of business and industry solutions, offering unparalleled speed, simplicity, and efficiency. A clean core in SAP S/4HANA ensures that businesses can leverage real-time data processing and analytics, essential for informed decision-making and competitive advantage.

 

The Imperative of SAP Clean Core

A clean core is essential for businesses to adapt swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer expectations. It enables organizations to leverage the latest SAP product advancements and integrate new software releases promptly. Without a clean core, businesses face challenges such as increased operational costs, inflexibility, and technical debt, which can hinder innovation and growth.

 

 

Without a clean core, organizations may encounter several challenges:

Technical Challenges

  • Complex Upgrades: Customizations can complicate the upgrade process, leading to longer and more costly system updates
  • Reduced System Performance: A cluttered core with custom code can degrade system performance and reliability
  • Increased Technical Debt: Over time, customizations can accumulate, resulting in technical debt that hampers agility and innovation.
  • Integration Difficulties: Custom code can create dependencies that make it difficult to integrate with other systems or technologies.

Operational Challenges

  • Operational Instability: A non-standard core may lead to unpredictable system behavior and increased downtime
  • Compliance Risks: Deviations from standard configurations can pose risks to regulatory compliance and data integrity
  • Resource Intensiveness: Customized systems require more resources for maintenance and support

Strategic Challenges

  • Inhibiting Digital Transformation: A dirty core can hinder an organization’s ability to adapt to new business models and technologies.
  • Barriers to Innovation: Excessive customizations can limit the ability to leverage new SAP functionalities and innovations.
  • Change Management Difficulties: Transitioning to a clean core requires significant change management efforts, which can be challenging if the existing system is heavily customized.

In summary, not maintaining a clean core can lead to increased costs, reduced efficiency, and a lack of flexibility, which are detrimental to an organization’s digital transformation journey. It’s essential to adopt a strategic approach to ensure a clean core for long-term success and sustainability.

Organizations looking to clean up their SAP core can start by adopting a strategic approach that includes the following steps:

  1. Assessment of Customizations: Begin with a thorough assessment of the existing system to identify all customizations, modifications, and enhancement.
  2. Fit-to-Standard Analysis: Evaluate the necessity of each customization against the standard SAP S/4HANA capabilities. Retire any custom code that is no longer needed or can be replaced by standard functionality.
  3. Leverage SAP Best Practices: Adopt SAP best practices and guided configurations to align with standard processes.
  4. Use of SAP BTP for Extensions: Utilize SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to build extensions rather than modifying the core system.
  5. API-First Approach: Implement an API-first strategy to facilitate integration without altering the core system.
  6. Continuous Monitoring and Optimization: Establish a process for ongoing monitoring and optimization to ensure the core remains clean and aligned with business needs.
  7. Documentation and Governance: Maintain comprehensive documentation of all changes and establish strong governance to manage technical debt.

 

By following these steps, organizations can systematically clean up their SAP core, leading to a more streamlined, efficient, and agile system that is better equipped for the digital transformation journey.

 Accelerating the Journey to a Clean Core with AI powered Automation

KTern.AI stands out as a digital transformation suite that aids in achieving a clean SAP core It offers digital tools for assessment, governance, process orchestration, testing, and optimization. With KTern.AI, businesses can plan risk-free SAP transformations, ensure productive governance, orchestrate SAP process lifecycles, and avoid disruptions during releases.

In conclusion, maintaining a clean core is not just a technical necessity; it is a strategic imperative that underpins the digital transformation journey. By embracing SAP Activate methodology, RISE with SAP, and SAP S/4HANA, and leveraging tools like KTern.AI, businesses can navigate the complexities of digital transformation with confidence and clarity


For more reading: 

@https://blogs.sap.com/2023/07/01/clean-core-demystified-what-does-it-mean-and-how-to-achieve-it-with... 

@ https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud... 

.

entry.title: S/4 HANA Public Cloud: Event-Based Costing - Costing Sheet and Activity Types
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community, 

 

Is it possible on SAP S/4 HANA Cloud to exclude the use of Activity Type for Labor Costs in production costing?

This is with the idea of using Costing Sheets fully for all production costs both in actual and standard costing for Labor and Overhead costs. 

How can we apply? 

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Can you provide suggestions for migrating data from SAP R/3 (4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (public)?
entry.content:
Hi ,
Aside from the staging table approach, can data migration be achieved using standard iFlows in SAP Cloud Platform Integration, or would we need to create custom iFlows or custom programs for this purpose?

Are there any other methods or third-party tools that can be used?

entry.title: Re-triggering Approval Flow Only on Price Increase for PO/Requisition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have a situation for both Purchase Orders (POs) and Purchase Requisitions (PRs) in our SAP MM module. Currently, when a change is made after a PO/PR has already been released, it re-triggers the approval flow regardless of the nature of the change.

We need to configure the system so that the approval flow is re-triggered only if the total PO/PR price has increased after the release.

Has anyone encountered a similar requirement and can provide guidance on how to achieve this?

Instead of using the traditional release procedure configuration, we are looking to leverage the Flexible Workflow. Specifically, we need:

  1. Guidance on setting up Flexible Workflow to re-trigger the approval process only when the total PO/PR price increases.
  2. Steps to configure conditions within the flexible workflow to handle this scenario.
  3. Any necessary BAdI implementations or enhancements to support this requirement.

Any detailed instructions, examples, or references to documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your assistance!

Best regards,
VIJAY RAWAL (SAP MM)

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition SAP Community  SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Sourcing and Procurement MM Purchasing #Release Strategy #BAdI (Business Add-Ins) #Release Strategy #Approval Process #Flexible Workflow

entry.title: Create business user API behavior
entry.content:
Hi,

I am creating business user using below API

querybusinessuserin


It creates business user. Also employee is getting created with above payload.

Is this expected behavior?

What change is needed if we don't want employee to get created with business user?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to create technical users in SAP cloud
entry.content:
How to create technical user and assign them roles for computer or system? It is required to distinguish users between technical users and business users. And currently I only found business roles in the system.

For example, there is a computer and everyone use this computer can logon the SAP system.

Another situation is that OA system can do some activities in SAP system, and it is not employee.

So how can I create user ID for these 2 situations?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Mapping Screens to Fields in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am new to the SAP S/4 HANA world and trying to transition into the technical field. My background is in SAP B1, where SAP provides excellent documentation that helps map screens to tables and fields.

In SAP S/4 HANA, I am finding it challenging to understand the mapping process. For example, if I have a specific screen, how can I identify the fields that might be mapped to either custom CDS views or public endpoints?

I am feeling a bit lost and would greatly appreciate your support and guidance on this matter.

entry.title: Renumber G/L Accounts App - use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).
entry.content:
When we try to Renumber G/L Accounts App  in out Partner Test and development system customising tenant, we get error:

To create a mapping, use the development system (3SL) or Q system (2SL).

Could you please help us to find a solution how to renumber G/L accounts?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How do I extract data from SAP R/3 (4.7) ?
entry.content:
Hi,

As we are trying to migrate data from SAP R/3 (Version 4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public) through staging tables, could you please suggest possible ways to extract data from version 4.7? We need to extract all fields and tables (both specified and unspecified/classified and non-classified) from R/3.

We have attempted to extract data using transaction code SE16N, but we couldn't extract all tables, especially the classified ones. By using transaction code RSO2 for custom data source creation, we managed to extract some data, but not all fields from the tables. Kindly suggest possible ways to achieve complete data extraction, including any other transaction codes that may be useful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Enabling SSO for SAP BTP UI5 CAPM Application with S/4 HANA Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I'm new to the SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition field and eager to learn as much as possible. I need your expertise on a specific scenario: enabling Single Sign-On (SSO) for my SAP BTP UI5 CAPM application hosted on Cloud Foundry, integrated with S/4 HANA Public Edition via Cloud Identity Services.

Article 1

Article 2

Article 3

I've come across various articles on this topic, but they mostly focus on on-premise S/4 HANA and BTP applications. If you have any articles, step-by-step guides, or video tutorials, I would greatly appreciate it if you could share them.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,
Rahul Jain

entry.title: Unable to navigate any provision to migration DMC projects from Dev env to Quality environment
entry.content:
Transport functionality is not there for Migrate your data application.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Switch ON Joule In TDD
entry.content:
Hi 

I would like to get the Joule functionality in our system - SAP S/HANA Public Cloud Edition.

Mainly to explore and know how to use it and demo for potential customers

Thank you 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Delta configurationfor Standard API in S/4 HANA cloud
entry.content:
In order to Create Delta for standard API:

Go to product master standard API in Business Accelerator Hub then click on tryout option.

 

Now, we have to configure our system environment here as shown below

 

 

 

 

 

And after successfully configuring Now you can tryout any of these operations in Standard API

 

Now in order to get delta, we have to take a delta pointer based on the fields in standard API. In this case I’m taking Lastchangedate field as delta pointer

 

And Filter out records based on delta pointer(Lastchangedate)as shown below

 

Here the logic is:   LastChangeDate ge datetime'2024-01-14T00:00:00’ And it says lastchangedate is greater than or equals to 2024-01-14 and also there are other logics as well: LastChangeDate ge datetime'2024-06-13T00:00:00' and LastChangeDate lt datetime'2024-06-14T00:00:00' and it only gives records between 14th june to 13th june .

So After initializing this logic run as shown below

 

we get records that are changed on or after 14th January 2024 in OData URL itself and it gives count of how many records are present as shown below

 

Now we can check data by taking URL till highlighted part and run OData in PowerBI

Now in Power BI, Select OData feed and add the required URL

And we can see required delta records here
